consoleFaqs.question: What is Taypro Console?
consoleFaqs.answer: Taypro Console is the secure web portal Taypro customers use to monitor and manage their Solar Panel Cleaning Robot fleets across one or many solar sites. It brings together live operational data, schedules, historical cleaning records, performance analytics, and support workflows, so plant and O&M teams can run robotic cleaning with the same rigour they expect from any critical plant system.

consoleFaqs.question: How do we get access to Taypro Console?
consoleFaqs.answer: Taypro provisions each client organisation with private access to the production portal. Your account team shares the login link, credentials, and onboarding pack outside this public site. For new deployments, start from our contact form; existing customers can request additional seats, sites, or password help via service@taypro.in.

consoleFaqs.question: Which Taypro robots work with Taypro Console?
consoleFaqs.answer: Taypro Console is the common fleet layer for Taypro autonomous and semi-automatic cleaning robots, including Model-A (fixed / seasonal tilt), Model-B (pick-and-place), and Model-T (single-axis trackers). The exact screens and commands available depend on your site layout, connectivity, and service package.

consoleFaqs.question: Can we schedule cleaning from the portal?
consoleFaqs.answer: Yes. Client teams can define and review block-wise automation schedules (timers) aligned with your plant’s operating rules and weather strategy. Execution still respects on-robot safety interlocks, the portal plans and requests; the fleet executes within engineered limits.

consoleFaqs.question: What kind of reporting does Taypro Console provide?
consoleFaqs.answer: Taypro Console surfaces site-level and block-wise cleaning coverage, robot battery and execution status, downloadable cleaning reports for audit and internal review, and statistics views for trends over time. Export formats and retention policies are aligned with your enterprise needs during onboarding.

consoleFaqs.question: How does Taypro Console connect to robots in the field?
consoleFaqs.answer: Depending on each plant’s network design, fleet telemetry and commands travel over secure links such as ${tayproRobotConnectivitySummary}. Taypro engineers size the gateway and backhaul architecture during deployment so Console stays responsive even on large, multi-block sites.

consoleFaqs.question: How do we get help if something looks wrong in the data?
consoleFaqs.answer: Taypro Console includes structured ticketing so your site administrators can raise issues, attach evidence, and track resolution with Taypro’s service team. For urgent operational events, your contract may also include direct escalation channels outside the portal.

capabilityCards.title: Site dashboards
capabilityCards.body: At-a-glance view of weather context, block-wise cleaning coverage, gateway connectivity, and robot battery readiness so operators can plan shifts with confidence.

capabilityCards.title: Robot configuration & device layout
capabilityCards.body: Structured visibility into deployed robots, block allocation, and permitted operating parameters, tuned by Taypro during commissioning, then monitored by your team.

capabilityCards.title: Automation schedules
capabilityCards.body: Centralised view of block-wise timers and cleaning windows so night-time or post-production cycles stay aligned with plant rules and seasonal soiling patterns.

capabilityCards.title: Cleaning logs & exports
capabilityCards.body: Historical cleaning activity, downloadable reports for selected dates, and tabbed views for completed runs, in-progress work, errors, and offline assets, built for audit-ready O&M.

capabilityCards.title: Statistics & trends
capabilityCards.body: Charts for area cleaned, blocks serviced, execution mix, and efficiency indicators, filterable by site, time range, and (where licensed) robot or block.

capabilityCards.title: Controlled robot commands
capabilityCards.body: A curated, permissioned command set for day-to-day operations, always confirmed, always logged. Deep engineering actions remain with Taypro service engineers.

capabilityCards.title: Fleet tracking context
capabilityCards.body: Live and last-known robot positioning on site layouts where available, ideal for coordination with security, O&M convoys, and incident response.

capabilityCards.title: Gateway & field telemetry health
capabilityCards.body: Visibility into gateway and field-device status so you can distinguish a true robot fault from a communications brownout before dispatching crews.

capabilityCards.title: Tickets & service history
capabilityCards.body: Raise, comment on, and close Taypro service tickets with a full timeline, fewer ad-hoc email threads, more traceability for your internal governance.
